# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure VMs with public IP addresses for direct access. Assign public IPs to each VM so developers can RDP/SSH directly without setting up a Bastion host or VPN. This simplifies our development environment setup.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0
parameters:
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: '[resourceGroup().location]'
    metadata:
      description: 'Location for all resources.'
  vmNamePrefix:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'devvm'
    metadata:
      description: 'Prefix for the VM names.'
  vmCount:
    type: int
    defaultValue: 1
    minValue: 1
    maxValue: 5
    metadata:
      description: 'Number of VMs to create (1 to 5).'
  adminUsername:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'azureuser'
    metadata:
      description: 'Username for the Virtual Machines.'
  adminPassword:
    type: securestring
    metadata:
      description: 'Password for the Virtual Machines.'
  vmSize:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'Standard_DS1_v2'
    metadata:
      description: 'Size of the virtual machines.'
  osType:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'Windows'
    allowedValues:
      - 'Windows'
      - 'Linux'
    metadata:
      description: 'Type of OS for the VMs (Windows or Linux).'
  windowsImageURN:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'MicrosoftWindowsServer:WindowsServer:2019-Datacenter:latest'
    metadata:
      description: 'URN for the Windows OS image (Publisher:Offer:Sku:Version).'
  linuxImageURN:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'Canonical:UbuntuServer:18.04-L
